Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: MAJOR DISCOVERY! Board layout changes based on player position. At col 50, there are 10 green blocks in a different configuration. Different player positions reveal different block sets.
- Goal model: Navigate player to all positions and collect all green blocks across all views to complete the level.
- Plan: Collect all 10 visible green blocks now, then explore other player positions for more blocks.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All 10 green blocks collected, purple=29. Level not completed. The board layout changed again - now there are no green blocks visible, just the light blue area with the player at the right edge.
The layout changed when I moved the player to col 50. Now after collecting all blocks, the layout is different again. I need to explore more positions.
Let me try moving LEFT to see if different positions reveal different sets of green blocks. The player is at col 50, let me move LEFT to explore other positions.
Actually, I need to reconsider the game mechanics. The purple bar is at 29, and game over is at 64. I have 35 actions left. I've already used 94 actions total.
Let me try moving LEFT to see if new green blocks appear at different positions.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Board layout changes with player position. Collected 10 blocks at col 50, purple=29. No green blocks visible now. Need to explore other positions.
Goal model: Find all positions that reveal green blocks and collect them all.
Plan: Move LEFT to explore different positions and find more green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move LEFT to explore
result = action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
player = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b'][0]
boundary = player['boundary']
player_cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
player_col = sum(player_cols) // len(player_cols)
purple = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p'][0]
print(f"Player col: {player_col}, Greens: {len(greens)}, Purple: {purple['pixels']}")

# If greens found, collect them
if greens:
    clicks = []
    for g in greens:
        boundary = g['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
        center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
        clicks.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_row, 'col': center_col})
    
    result = action(clicks)
    print(f"Collected {len(greens)} blocks. Level completed: {result.get('level_completed')}")
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
    purple = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p'][0]
    print(f"Remaining greens: {len(greens)}, Purple: {purple['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
